world_assessment: Quiet macro week with no major catalysts. Key data points: AMD launching 'Milan' data center chip against Intel (net positive for TSMC as AMD's foundry partner), Cascend raising MU price target to $110 (direct analyst confirmation of supercycle thesis), and multiple portfolio names hitting 52-week highs (CAT, GS, F, BA). The reflation trade continues to work — industrials, energy, and financials leading while clean energy remains technically broken across the board, overwhelmed by rising rate headwinds despite favorable Biden policy backdrop. We're trailing the S&P by 0.8% with 41% cash — very modest deployment warranted, but trade discipline is critical given we're already at 18 trades for the simulation. No meaningful macro surprises this week; the market is digesting the prior week's stimulus passage and grinding higher on reopening optimism.

thesis_updates.notes: AMD Milan chip news is net positive — AMD uses TSMC for fabrication, so AMD gaining data center share vs Intel increases TSMC fab utilization. Structurally bullish. Technically weak this week (RSI=45, below SMA50, MACD bearish, OBV falling) — the market is rotating out of growth-adjacent semi names in the rate-rising environment. Structural thesis unchanged. Watching for RSI to recover above 50 and reclaim SMA50 before considering adding.

Digital banking innovation article confirms JPM is not ceding fintech ground — they are building into disruption rather than being disrupted by it. +12.9% gain. RSI=61, above SMA50, OBV rising. MACD turned bearish which is a mild timing caution. Thesis intact and performing well. No changes needed.

Short thesis under modest technical pressure — MACD has turned bullish and OBV rising are near-term headwinds. However, ADX=15 indicates weak directional trend and stock remains below SMA50, which keeps the short viable per our belief framework. Structural bear case (5G capex burden, T-Mobile competitive pressure, cord-cutting acceleration) unchanged. Monitor for any sustained reclaim of SMA50, which would be the key invalidation trigger.

Cascend maintained Buy and raised PT to $110 — direct analyst confirmation of our $105 target and supercycle thesis. RSI=56, above SMA50. MACD bearish is a timing caution but fundamentals are strengthening. +13.3% gain. Thesis intact.

Hit 52-week high this week. RSI=64, MACD bullish, OBV rising, ADX=34. Infrastructure spending thesis playing out in price action. +16.4% gain. No changes — strongest technical picture in the industrial cluster.

+25.3% gain, our top performer. RSI=72 is approaching overbought territory. MACD bullish, OBV rising, ADX=34. Thesis intact and strongest expression of the agriculture + infrastructure reflationary thesis. Will watch RSI for trim signal above 75 but no action yet — strong ADX trend suggests overbought can extend.
Hit 52-week high. RSI=65, MACD bullish, OBV rising, ADX=35. Capital markets boom and M&A activity thesis playing out. +20.7% gain. No changes needed.

Hit 52-week high. RSI=56, MACD bullish, ADX=44 (strongest trend in portfolio), OBV flat. EV transition thesis intact. +15.3% gain. OBV flat is a mild watch item but ADX=44 confirms institutional conviction in the trend.
RSI=72, approaching overbought. MACD bullish, OBV rising, ADX=37. Infrastructure steel thesis playing out. +11.5% gain. Watch RSI for trim signal above 75 but no action yet.

RSI=60, above SMA50, OBV rising, ADX=42. MACD turned bearish — mild timing caution. Commodity inflation and oil demand recovery thesis intact. +5.4% gain. The OBV rising with strong ADX suggests institutional holders are not exiting despite MACD signal.

Recent addition showing +2.7%. MACD bearish, OBV falling — early technical warning that needs monitoring. Rising rate NIM expansion thesis is structurally intact. If OBV continues falling through next week, will reconsider whether the position has early-stage thesis slippage.

new_positions.thesis: Mastercard is the cleanest expression of the Digital Finance Revolution theme without overlapping our existing banking (JPM, GS) or brokerage (SCHW) positions. Payment networks are the toll roads of the digital economy — they take a fee on every transaction with no credit risk, no balance sheet risk, and no capital intensity. As consumer spending recovers with vaccine rollout acceleration, transaction volumes flow directly to MA's revenue. Cross-border fees (highest margin revenue line) recover as international travel resumes — this is a direct reopening catalyst layered on top of the secular digital payments thesis. The network effect is irreplaceable: 3 billion cards, 100+ million merchant locations, two-sided marketplace that compounds. Cash displacement to digital payments is a multi-decade secular trend with 2021 reopening providing a near-term catalyst. RSI=67, above SMA50, MACD bullish, OBV rising — technically sound with institutional momentum confirming the entry.
new_positions.invalidation: Consumer spending data deteriorates materially, cross-border volumes fail to recover through H2 2021, or RSI falls below 50 with MACD turning bearish indicating trend failure.
new_positions.timing_note: RSI=67, above SMA50, MACD bullish, OBV rising — entering in confirmed uptrend with reopening volume catalyst approaching

theme_updates.name: Clean Energy Transition
theme_updates.reason: Decrementing to 0 removes this theme. All clean energy names remain technically broken six weeks into the rising rate environment (ENPH RSI=48 below SMA50, FSLR RSI=45 below SMA50, NEE RSI=46 below SMA50, VST RSI=41 below SMA50 — all with OBV falling). Three positions stopped out (ENPH, NEE, and indirectly FCX as the copper-electrification play). Biden policy tailwinds are real but rate-driven multiple compression is overwhelming them on a 3-6 month horizon. The theme is structurally valid for 2022-2025 but not actionable in the current rate environment. Will revisit when 10-year yield stabilizes or clean energy technicals recover above SMA50.

lesson_updates.reason: Clean energy continues to underperform systematically (ENPH stopped out ~$41 below entry, NEE stopped out ~$11 below entry, all solar names technically broken) while financials (JPM +12.9%, GS +20.7%) and cyclicals (CAT +16.4%, DE +25.3%) continue to outperform. This is now a confirmed, repeating pattern across 8+ weeks of simulation data with no counter-evidence. Rising rates are bifurcating the market exactly as described. Incrementing to 4/5.

weekly_summary: Quiet week with thin news flow but portfolio continues to compound the reflation trade — CAT, GS, and F all hit 52-week highs, DE at +25% is our top performer and NUE/MU adding to gains. AMD's Milan chip launch is net positive for TSMC (AMD is a TSMC fab customer). Cascend's MU price target raise to $110 confirms the memory supercycle thesis. Added MA to express Digital Finance Revolution through the payment network toll-road model — complements existing banking/brokerage positions without overlap. Removed Clean Energy Transition theme as rising rates are systematically compressing clean energy multiples with no investable near-term setup; all three clean energy positions stopped out and all names remain technically broken. Trailing S&P by only 0.8% with 41% cash — one selective deployment into high-quality name with clean technicals. Will continue disciplined pacing given elevated trade count.
